spec_match.rationale: Highlights the visual/structural mismatch (h1 looks like h2 but a11y tree says level 1) — the core motivation.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises sighted-vs-screen-reader contrast; the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't address the historically-incorrect document-outline-algorithm rationale or how the deprecation interacts with aria-level overrides.

openQuestions.title: Show aria-level interaction
openQuestions.detail: Some authors used `aria-level` to force the right level. Demo what happens when aria-level conflicts with the implicit h1 level.

openQuestions.title: Pair with a real screen-reader recording
openQuestions.detail: Link or embed an actual NVDA / VoiceOver clip reading the broken document. The visual comparison plus audio of the mismatch makes the case unanswerably.

openQuestions.title: Add 'fix' button
openQuestions.detail: After showing the mismatch, offer a button that rewrites h1->h2 (or vice versa) so the visitor sees the recommended remediation.

summary: Solid motivation demo. To strengthen, add aria-level overlap and a real screen-reader sample so the a11y argument has audio receipts.
